# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Function that streams responses to clients. Accept user-provided content and headers from POST body and stream them back using ReadableStream and Response. This enables our real-time data streaming API at the edge. Use the Web Streams API to pipe user data directly to the response.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// Import necessary libraries 
import { Readable } from 'stream';
import { IncomingMessage, ServerResponse } from "http";
  
export default function (req: IncomingMessage & { body?: string }, res: ServerResponse) : void | Promise<ReadableStream>{    // Typecasting to avoid any type mismatch error. 
     try {        
        if (!(req instanceof IncomingMessage)) throw new Error('Invalid request object');   // Check for valid incoming message instance, in case of invalid instances it will not work as expected and may cause runtime errors or unexpected behavior on the server side too (like throwing an exception).   
         
       const stream = async () => { 
         if (!req.body) throw new Error('Request body is missing');   // Check for valid request content, in case of invalid requests it will not work as expected and may cause runtime errors or unexpected behavior on the server side too (like throwing an exception).   
         
        const readable = new ReadableStream({ 
            start(controller) {    
                controller.enqueue('User Content: ' + req.body);   // Enqueues user content into stream, you can replace this with any logic to generate your data source (like a database query or file reading).   
                
               if(!req.headers['content-length'])  {    
                   controller.error(new Error('Content length not provided'));     
                } else{   // Ensures content is fully consumed before sending the response, in case of incomplete data it will cause a runtime error or unexpected behavior on client side too (like exceeding memory limit).   
                    if ((req as any).readableLength !== parseInt(<string> req.headers['content-length'])) {   // Checks content length against received headers, in case of mismatch it will not work correctly and may cause runtime errors or unexpected behavior on the client side too (like exceeding memory limit/exceeded stream buffer size).   
                        controller.close();    
                    } else{ 
                       res.end(`Data Streamed Successfully! \n User Content: ${req.body} `);   // Ends response with success message and streams user content back to client, you can replace this part as per your requirement (like sending a file or JSON object).   
                    } 
                }}    
        });     
         res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'text/event-stream');   // Sets the response header for SSE(Server Sent Events) which is used to stream data back from server side and client can start receiving it in real time as soon as a new event arrives (like user content).   
          return readable;  }    
         res.writeHead(200);   // Writes HTTP status code for successful request, you may want this if your requirement is to check the server's response or not able to write head in some cases like during testing environment setup (like setting headers).   
          stream().pipe(res)  ;     // Pipes readable data into writable res object.  
      } catch {      
         console.error('An error occurred while processing the request');        // Logs any errors that occur for debugging purposes, you may want to replace this with a more robust logging system in production environment (like using winston or similar).   
          if(res && res.writable) {   // Checks whether response is writable and closes it once an error occurs while processing the request so that further requests can't be processed on top of this stream anymore, you may want to replace with a more robust system in production environment (like closing connections or sending HTTP 406).   
              res.end();   // Ends response if writable is not available for some reason like during testing environments setup and when using writeHead method which does nothing but throws an error, you may want to replace this with a more robust system in production environment (like closing connections or sending HTTP 406).   
          } else {   // If res.writable was false then it means that the response is not writable and can't be closed anymore so no action will happen here, you may want to replace this with a more robust system in production environment (like closing connections or sending HTTP 406).   
              return;     }   // Ends function if res.writable was false as per above reasoning for not being able write head and also close the connection when it's writable is set to true but still unable send response due some other reasons (like exceeding memory limit/exceeded stream buffer size).   
      };  }   // End of try catch block.
